# UC-23.9.4 · Water Consumption Monitoring and Conservation

## Description

Correlates per-facility potable-water telemetry from BACnet/IP building gateways, Modbus TCP pulse counters, cellular AMI interval exports (Itron Sensus Badger-class heads), campus xWMS boundary balances, and SCADA tower makeup streams into daily then rolling-seven-day gallons per facility_id and tenant_id; applies AWWA M36-style bookkeeping separating tagged transmission-or-service real-loss metres from authorized branches; flags idle-period leak suspicion versus irrigation-heavy weekdays; joins drought ordinance tiers via water_restriction_tier.csv; and yields disclosure-ready gallon narratives aligned with CDP Water Security, GRI 303 water disclosures, and ESRS E3 tables—without mistaking reclaimed-purple deliveries for theft.

## Value

Water insecurity already touches roughly four billion people at least one month each year in UN World Water Development Report narratives while tariff cliffs during drought tiers punish apparel, food-and-beverage, semiconductor fabs, hospital campuses, and hyperscale districts that disclose gallon intensity to banks and insurers. CDP Water Security disclosure became mandatory for more than eighteen thousand organizations representing over seven trillion dollars in procurement spend—finance expects Splunk-ready gallon reconciliations against municipal JDBC invoices before attestations freeze. Facilities leaders gain priced transparency into surcharge ladders instead of debating unexplained wholesale invoices weeks after penalties post.

## Implementation

Normalize `_time` from meter clocks (not indexer receipt); tag meter_role during field extraction; publish water_restriction_tier.csv plus optional facility_occupancy_profile.csv; route Splunk HTTP Event Collector tokens per DMZ VLAN; schedule the weekly saved search for drought posture and daily clone for idle-leak suspicion; archive CSV evidence for CDP questionnaire windows.

## Detailed Implementation

Prerequisites
• Splunk Enterprise 9.2+ or Splunk Cloud roles covering index=facilities plus optional index=iot_sensors for collars and consolidated AMI staging indexes when cellular exporters bypass campus VLAN naming.
• Heavy Forwarder or DMZ collector sized for fifteen-minute AMI CSV bursts over HTTP Event Collector with indexer acknowledgement enabled when LTE exporters pause-and-resume mid-batch.
• Governance memo freezing meter_role vocabulary for district_feed versus irrigation versus nrw_real branches—duplicate tagging doubles gallons and collapses AWWA M36 decomposition.
• Lookup water_restriction_tier.csv published under Settings → Lookups → Lookup table files with transforms.conf stanza water_restriction_tier keyed facility_id supplying drought_stage, weekly_limit_gal, ordinance_version sourced from municipality PDF ordinances.
• JDBC path for Splunk DB Connect to the warehouse mirror of utility billing extracts so jdbc_water_billing rows reconcile AMI sums monthly.

**Step 1 — Configure data collection**

Mint Splunk HTTP Event Collector tokens via Settings → Data inputs → HTTP Event Collector per DMZ VLAN when BACnet gateways cannot share subnets with AMI exporters. Stamp _time from meter firmware clocks so daily bins match civic curfew windows rather than indexer lag around daylight-saving pivots.

Normalize BACnet/IP publisher JSON so analogInput identifiers stay unique across Johnson Controls Metasys, Honeywell ALN, Schneider EcoStruxure, and Trane Tracer stacks before sourcetype bacnet:water_meter events land—collision between irrigation analogValue and utility-main analogInput double-counts gallons until browse exports re-key object tables.

Poll Modbus TCP port 502 function code 03 where paired holding registers encode thirty-two-bit IEEE cumulative gallons—decode endian order per OEM briefs before converting gal_pulse increments; endian drift inflates apparent-loss math an order of magnitude.

Route OpenWay Collection Engine REST or MQTT fifteen-minute AMI batches (Itron-class), Sensus FlexNet XML/CSV drops, and Badger BEACON API pulls into distinct sourcetypes sharing common field names for gallons_increment. Map xWMS district XML balance events to sourcetype xwms:district_boundary when consolidated master metres exist.

Forward chiller-yard SCADA historians (OPC-UA bridged through Kafka optional) into sourcetype scada:process_water with makeup_volume_gal or derived increment so tower circuits stay separable from domestic risers.

Schedule Splunk DB Connect (Splunkbase 2686) nightly JDBC against the finance warehouse staging table that mirrors billed gallons; emit jdbc_water_billing with invoiced_gallons and billing_period_end for Step 3 invoice-to-telemetry parity.

Post IoT acoustic collars to sourcetype leak_sensor:event under index=iot_sensors for secondary correlation—not as a primary gallon numerator—so leak escalations annotate suspect_idle_leak without fabricating litre math from alarm heartbeats alone.

Optional Splunk Industrial Asset Intelligence (Splunkbase 4347) accelerates historian-to-Splunk bridges. Optional Splunk OT Security Add-on (Splunkbase 5234) documents DMZ collectors when segmented OT historians cross firewalls toward enterprise search heads.

**Step 2 — Create the search and alert**

Paste the SPL from the spl field into saved search water_facility_weekly_m36_proxy_v1. Schedule weekly earliest=-30d@d for tier compliance and clone daily earliest=-14d for leak chase on suspect_idle_leak.

The search coalesce() harmonises vendor keys; bin span=1d rolls interval reads to civil days; production_gal represents system input volume analogues via district_feed or xwms_boundary tags; authorized_gal captures authorised branch consumption including cooling makeup; real_loss_gal rolls explicit loss tags (transmission_loss, distribution_leak, storage_overflow, service_line_leak, nrw_real) when instrumentation exists; apparent_loss_gal enforces AWWA M36 non-negative remainder after subtracting authorised and tagged real metres. suspect_idle_leak flags weekend low-occupancy bleed heuristics—tighten by joining facility_occupancy_profile.csv when badge feeds exist.

Throttle drought_violation by facility across seventy-two hours because legitimate irrigation commissioning can exceed weekly quotas momentarily while controllers learn.

Do not duplicate the SPL string here—the spl field remains the single authoritative copy to avoid drift between saved searches and documentation.

**Step 3 — Validate**

Select three pilots mixing BACnet-rich towers, pure-AMI warehouses, and one district with xWMS boundary balance feeds. Compare Splunk weekly_gal to BEACON SaaS CSV or Itron Total Meter Manager extracts for identical billing intervals; investigate variances beyond six percent for timezone drift, double counting, or rollover miscoding.

Cross-check jdbc_water_billing invoiced_gallons against PDF utility invoices referencing master-meter account numbers before CDP Water Security submissions quote Splunk totals.

Spot-check BACnet analogInput IDs against commissioning binders—verify irrigation iso-valve tags versus potable bundles.

Compare drought_stage text in lookups to downloadable city drought PDFs archived with Legal—stale ordinance_version rows wreck defensibility overnight.

Extend JDBC wastewater metre extracts separately when ESRS E3 discharge narratives demand paired withdrawals—this UC scopes potable volumetrics unless sibling instrumentation feeds arrive.

**Step 4 — Operationalize**

Publish Dashboard Studio layouts referenced under visualization—default ninety-day picker for drought storytelling plus thirty-day picker for Ops leak drills.

Route drought_violation alerts first to Sustainability shared inbox carbon-copy Facilities chief engineer—suppress executive paging until ordinance overlays plus NFPA churn exclusions validate breaches.

Archive weekly CSV extracts plus SHA256 hashes into SharePoint folders aligned to disclosure calendars with reviewer initials referencing methodology attachments bundled beside Splunk screenshots.

Segregate Knowledge Manager lookup-edit rights from DB Connect SQL authoring so JDBC extracts cannot broaden unexpectedly into payroll schemas.

Quarterly tabletop rehearsals tying Splunk gallons to finance spreadsheets bridging GRI 303-3 withdrawal totals remind assurance teams meter-grounded numerators precede narrative polishing.

**Step 5 — Troubleshooting**

• Basement AMI radios dropping LTE Cat-M1 connectivity yield twelve-to-twenty-four-hour ingestion gaps masking overnight hosebib bleed—overlay differential-pressure trending sensors or correlate leak_sensor:event escalation streams before trusting silent AMI nights alone.

• BACnet instance divergence across Trane Tracer versus Schneider EcoStruxure naming conventions routes irrigation pulses through district_feed regex buckets inflating apparent losses until canonical BACnet browse exports remap analogInput IDs against CAD iso-tag spreadsheets—reload transforms after Facilities uploads CSV corrections under Settings → Lookups → Lookup definitions.

• Irrigation superintendent manually overrides drought timers during turf-brown ordinances producing lawful gallons spikes Splunk reads as violations until ordinance_calendar.csv annotates exemption tokens tied to civic variance permits issued weekly.

• Cooling-tower conductivity-controlled blowdown fifteen-minute duty cycles mimic concealed leakage saw-tooth signatures—join conductivity_probe historian sourcetype rows when chemistries justify bursts versus unexplained rises.

• Hybrid workforce shrinking weekday occupancy thirty-to-sixty percent without revising gallons-per-occupant denominators disguises behavioural drift—refresh facility_details.csv occupant counts quarterly using IWMS badge reconciliation feeds paired with this UC.

• Saturday/Sunday continuous gallon totals exceeding five hundred while authorised branch totals fall below one hundred strongly imply toilet-flapper or stuck irrigation valve leaks deserving ultrasonic leak detector dispatch workflows outlined in Facilities CMMS—not Splunk silence.

• Q3 evaporative cooling makeup gallons routinely peak five-to-tenfold above January troughs reflecting lawful evaporative loss—not leakage—until chillers idle confirm magnitude aligns with cooling-degree-day overlays sourced from NOAA feeds blended alongside Splunk gallons.

• Eight-digit cumulative registers wrapping nine-nine-nine-nine-nine-nine-nine-nine gallons flip negative deltas Splunk theft detectors misconstrue until pipelines insert conditional arithmetic resetting deltas across rollover boundaries documented per AWWA meter manuals.

• Pressure-reducing-valve diaphragm ruptures permit occasional reverse instantaneous vectors decrementing cumulative registers despite lawful evaporative consumption elsewhere—inspect differential gauges upstream/downstream PRV vault when Splunk negatives coincide with hammer complaints.

• Weekly NFPA 25 diesel fire-pump churn tests discharge hundreds of gallons through test headers resembling catastrophic bursts—suppress Splunk alerts using CMMS calendar webhook annotating permitted_flow_gpm tokens merged via lookup keyed on facility_id and NFPA_TEST_WINDOW boolean.

• Purple-pipe reclaimed laterals plumbed beside domestic bundles duplicate gallons when telemetry coefficients mismatch colour-coded pipe schedules—maintain reclaimed_water.csv tagging purple taps separately so potable dashboards exclude reclaim totals mandated by landscaping ordinances only counting purple deliveries.

• Cooling-tower drift losses commonly range one-to-three percent of recirculation mass flows ignored when evaporative calculations omit drift coefficients—engineering spreadsheets reconcile drift versus evaporative splits before blaming leakage dashboards fed purely by makeup metres alone.

• AWWA M36 apparent-loss decomposition miscategorises unmetered municipal park irrigation taps pulling upstream of billed campus metres—Splunk surfaces phantom theft though parks departments rightly withdraw unmetered municipal taps authorised elsewhere until GIS lateral diagrams annotate unmetered_landscape.csv overlays subtracted upstream.

• Municipal drought frameworks arguing customer-specific historical baselines versus uniform percentage reductions diverge mathematically—Splunk weekly_limit_gal rows sourced only from uniform tiers miscategorise compliant campuses whenever ordinances mandate proportional cuts tied to customised allotments referenced outside Splunk lookups until Legal publishes authoritative drought_stage clauses translated verbatim into CSV ordinance_version columns matched quarterly.

## SPL

```spl
(index=facilities (sourcetype="bacnet:water_meter" OR sourcetype="modbus:water_meter" OR sourcetype="itron:ami_meter" OR sourcetype="sensus:meter_read" OR sourcetype="badger:meter" OR sourcetype="xwms:district_balance" OR sourcetype="xwms:district_boundary" OR sourcetype="scada:process_water")) earliest=-30d@d latest=@d
| eval meter_id=coalesce(meter_id, ami_meter_id, bacnet_object_instance, external_meter_uid)
| eval facility_id=coalesce(facility_id, site, campus_key)
| eval tenant_id=coalesce(tenant_id, division_code, "portfolio")
| eval gal=tonumber(coalesce(gallons_increment, gal_delta, volume_gal, makeup_volume_gal, "0"))
| eval meter_role=lower(coalesce(meter_role, service_class, "general"))
| bin _time span=1d
| stats sum(gal) as daily_gal sum(eval(if(match(meter_role,"(district_feed|city_master|utility_main|xwms_boundary)"),gal,0))) as production_gal sum(eval(if(match(meter_role,"(submeter|cooling_makeup|irrigation|process_potable)"),gal,0))) as authorized_gal sum(eval(if(match(meter_role,"(transmission_loss|distribution_leak|storage_overflow|service_line_leak|nrw_real)"),gal,0))) as real_loss_gal by facility_id tenant_id _time
| eval apparent_loss_gal=max(production_gal-authorized_gal-real_loss_gal,0)
| eval dow=strftime(_time,"%w")
| eval day_kind=if(dow="0" OR dow="6","weekend","weekday")
| eval suspect_idle_leak=if(day_kind="weekend" AND daily_gal>500 AND authorized_gal<100,1,0)
| bin _time span=7d
| stats sum(daily_gal) as weekly_gal max(apparent_loss_gal) as wk_apparent max(real_loss_gal) as wk_real_loss max(suspect_idle_leak) as leak_flag by facility_id tenant_id _time
| lookup water_restriction_tier.csv facility_id OUTPUT drought_stage weekly_limit_gal
| eval drought_violation=if(isnotnull(weekly_limit_gal) AND weekly_gal>weekly_limit_gal,1,0)
| sort - weekly_gal
| table facility_id tenant_id _time weekly_gal wk_apparent wk_real_loss leak_flag drought_stage drought_violation
```

## Visualization

Dashboard Studio: line chart of trailing daily or weekly gallons by facility_id; gauge comparing weekly_gal to weekly_limit_gal from drought lookup; heatmap of average gallons by hour-of-day versus day-of-week for idle-flow diagnostics; Sankey diagram when mapping_lookup ties district_feed streams to irrigation, tenant, tower makeup end-use buckets; sortable table ranking highest apparent-loss intensity per thousand square feet when facility_details.csv supplies floor areas.
